title: Laxmi Dental launches AI-connected remote dental monitoring platform i Scope360
summary: Platform integrates aligner tracking, oral health assessment and virtual consultations into a single dental care ecosystem
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Platform integrates aligner tracking, oral health assessment and virtual consultations into a single dental care ecosystem

Laxmi Dental Limited today announced the launch of ‘i Scope360’, a fully integrated AI-connected remote dental monitoring ecosystem that combines aligner tracking, oral health assessment, and virtual consultations into one connected platform.

The launch is aimed at addressing operational, accessibility, and patient engagement challenges faced by the dental industry while making dental care more convenient, connected, and scalable for clinics and consumers. Developed and manufactured in India using dental-grade materials, the platform is designed to support digitally connected treatment workflows for Indian and global clinical requirements. The company estimates that i Scope360 can help reduce physical follow-up visits by up to 30–50 per cent and improve treatment compliance by nearly 20–30 per cent through remote monitoring, virtual supervision, and connected patient engagement.

Built around three integrated modules — iTrack, iHealth, and iTrack Live — the platform enables patients to upload guided scans through their smartphones while allowing dentists to monitor treatment progress, assess oral health concerns, and conduct virtual consultations remotely. The platform is designed to improve treatment visibility, monitoring continuity, and communication between clinics and patients throughout the treatment journey.

According to the company, the dental industry continues to face challenges including fragmented workflows, delayed interventions, inconsistent patient compliance, dependence on physical clinic visits, and limited access to specialist-led digital dentistry across several regions. Patients also face issues related to appointment scheduling, travel requirements, limited visibility into treatment progress, and missed follow-ups. The company stated that i Scope360 has been introduced to address these gaps through digitally connected monitoring and communication workflows.

Rajesh Khakhar said, “Digital dentistry is no longer a concept of the future but a necessary shift for improving access, efficiency, and patient engagement. With i Scope360, we are extending dental care beyond clinic walls and enabling a connected ecosystem where monitoring, guidance, and care can continue seamlessly from home. This reflects our commitment to building technology-led dental solutions that support patients in a more scalable and accessible manner.”

Sameer Merchant said, “The future of dentistry is moving toward connected and digitally enabled care models. With i Scope360, we aim to support clinics and patients through remote monitoring, oral health assessments, and virtual consultations integrated into one ecosystem. The product has been developed to improve treatment continuity, patient engagement, and accessibility while reducing dependence on frequent physical visits.”

The company stated that the iTrack module focuses on remote aligner monitoring through AI-guided scan submissions and treatment tracking. iHealth enables remote oral health assessments that support earlier visibility into oral health concerns, while iTrack Live integrates scheduled virtual consultations into the treatment workflow to support communication and patient supervision.

Unlike conventional teleconsultation platforms that focus on communication, i Scope360 has been developed specifically for dental treatment workflows by integrating monitoring, consultations, oral health assessments, patient progress visibility, and remote supervision into a single ecosystem. Through smartphone-based accessibility, automated reminders, scan tracking, and virtual consultations, the platform is intended to support treatment continuity and improve patient engagement across aligner and oral healthcare journeys.

The launch also aligns with the increasing adoption of digital orthodontics and remote monitoring technologies within dentistry, as clinics and patients move toward digitally connected treatment models and preventive oral healthcare systems.
